clusters.title: Belgian court sends ex-diplomat, 93, to trial over 1961 murder of Congo leader
clusters.neutral_headline: Belgian Court Sends Ex Diplomat To Trial

items.title: Belgian court sends ex-diplomat, 93, to trial over 1961 murder of Congo leader
items.standfirst: Family of then PM, Patrice Lumumba, welcome decision to charge Étienne Davignon as ‘beginning of a reckoning’ A former Belgian diplomat, 93, should stand trial over alleged complicity in the 1961 murder of Patrice Lumumba, the first prime minister of what was then the newly independent Congolese state, a Brussels court has ruled.Étienne Davignon, the only person still alive among 10 Belgians the Lumumba family accuses of involvement in the killing, is charged with participation in war crimes.The illegal transfer of Lumumba and his associates from Léopoldville (now Kinshasa) to Katanga.The “humiliating and degrading treatment” of the men.Depriving them of a fair trial. Continue reading...
items.content: Family of then PM, Patrice Lumumba, welcome decision to charge Étienne Davignon as ‘beginning of a reckoning’ A former Belgian diplomat, 93, should stand trial over alleged complicity in the 1961 murder of Patrice Lumumba, the first prime minister of what was then the newly independent Congolese state, a Brussels court has ruled.Étienne Davignon, the only person still alive among 10 Belgians the Lumumba family accuses of involvement in the killing, is charged with participation in war crimes.The illegal transfer of Lumumba and his associates from Léopoldville (now Kinshasa) to Katanga.The “humiliating and degrading treatment” of the men.Depriving them of a fair trial. Continue reading...

items.title: Ex-Belgian diplomat ordered to stand trial over murder of Congo’s Lumumba
items.standfirst: Etienne Davignon, 93, is the only one alive among 10 Belgians accused by the Congolese leader's family of complicity.
items.content: Etienne Davignon, 93, is the only one alive among 10 Belgians accused by the Congolese leader's family of complicity.

items.title: Belgian aristocrat to face charges over murder of Congo’s first premier
items.standfirst: Étienne Davignon, 93, is the only surviving former official accused in connection to Patrice Lumumba’s death
items.content: Étienne Davignon, 93, is the only surviving former official accused in connection to Patrice Lumumba’s death

items.title: Former Belgian diplomat first to stand trial over 1961 murder of Congo’s Lumumba
items.standfirst: A former Belgian diplomat has been ordered to stand trial over the 1961 murder of Patrice Lumumba, who became the first prime minister of Congo after the country’s independence from Belgium. The 93-year-old suspect is the first person to be prosecuted in the case. All other suspects have passed away.
items.content: A former Belgian diplomat has been ordered to stand trial over the 1961 murder of Patrice Lumumba, who became the first prime minister of Congo after the country’s independence from Belgium. The 93-year-old suspect is the first person to be prosecuted in the case. All other suspects have passed away.

items.title: Belgian court clears way for trial over 1961 killing of Congo PM Lumumba
items.standfirst: Etienne Davignon, a 93-year-old ex-diplomat, is accused of involvement in the assassination of the independence hero.
items.content: Etienne Davignon, a 93-year-old ex-diplomat, is accused of involvement in the assassination of the independence hero.

ai_summary: Étienne Davignon is 93 years old
Charged with participation in war crimes
Alleged complicity in 1961 murder of Patrice Lumumba
Illegal transfer of Lumumba and associates
Depriving them of a fair trial

clusters.title: Pakistan denies it targeted Kabul hospital, says it hit 'terrorist' targets

items.title: Pakistan denies it targeted Kabul hospital, says it hit 'terrorist' targets
items.standfirst: Pakistan on Monday said it had carried out precision strikes on "military installations and terrorist support infrastructure" in Kabul but denied Afghan claims that strikes hit a drug treatment centre, killing hundreds of civilians.
items.content: Pakistan on Monday said it had carried out precision strikes on "military installations and terrorist support infrastructure" in Kabul but denied Afghan claims that strikes hit a drug treatment centre, killing hundreds of civilians.

items.title: ‘Everything was burning, people were burning’: witnesses describe strike on Kabul drug rehab centre
items.standfirst: Pakistani strike on Afghan capital kills 400 people, who burned in their beds or were crushed by collapsing wallsWitnesses and survivors have described the horrific scenes of a Pakistani air raid that hit a drug rehabilitation centre in Kabul, killing more than 400 people, who burned in their beds or were crushed by the collapsing building.Afghan rescue crews were still digging bodies out of the rubble on Tuesday after the strike, the deadliest single attack so far in a three-week war between the two countries. Continue reading...
items.content: Pakistani strike on Afghan capital kills 400 people, who burned in their beds or were crushed by collapsing wallsWitnesses and survivors have described the horrific scenes of a Pakistani air raid that hit a drug rehabilitation centre in Kabul, killing more than 400 people, who burned in their beds or were crushed by the collapsing building.Afghan rescue crews were still digging bodies out of the rubble on Tuesday after the strike, the deadliest single attack so far in a three-week war between the two countries. Continue reading...

items.title: What happened in the Kabul hospital strike? #world
items.standfirst: The strike, the claims, and the consequences Rescue crews and Afghan officials recovered bodies from a drug rehabilitation clinic in Kabul after an overnight airstrike that hit the facility. Afghan authorities described an enormous civilian toll, saying hundreds were killed and many more injured.…
items.content: The strike, the claims, and the consequences Rescue crews and Afghan officials recovered bodies from a drug rehabilitation clinic in Kabul after an overnight airstrike that hit the facility. items.title: Afghanistan says 400 killed in strike by Pakistan on Kabul hospital
items.standfirst: Deputy government spokesman says death toll has reached 400 people ‘so far’ as Islamabad denies targeting facility for drug addictsHundreds were feared dead after a strike on a hospital treating drug users in the Afghan capital of Kabul, which officials from Afghanistan blamed on the Pakistani military.Afghanistan’s deputy government spokesperson Hamdullah Fitrat said the death toll had “so far” reached 400 people, while about 250 people had been reported injured. He said most of those killed and wounded were patients undergoing treatment at the facility. Continue reading...
items.content: Deputy government spokesman says death toll has reached 400 people ‘so far’ as Islamabad denies targeting facility for drug addictsHundreds were feared dead after a strike on a hospital treating drug users in the Afghan capital of Kabul, which officials from Afghanistan blamed on the Pakistani military.Afghanistan’s deputy government spokesperson Hamdullah Fitrat said the death toll had “so far” reached 400 people, while about 250 people had been reported injured. He said most of those killed and wounded were patients undergoing treatment at the facility. Continue reading...

items.title: Afghanistan claims 400 killed by Pakistan in strike on Kabul 'drug treatment hospital'
items.standfirst: Afghanistan has claimed 400 people have been killed in a Pakistan strike which Kabul said hit a hospital that treats drug addicts.
items.content: Afghanistan has claimed 400 people have been killed in a Pakistan strike which Kabul said hit a hospital that treats drug addicts.

ai_summary: Pakistan carried out precision strikes
Denied hitting a drug treatment centre
Afghan claims say hundreds of civilians killed
Strike hit a drug rehabilitation centre
More than 400 people killed in the strike

items.title: Former White House chief of staff Andy Card weighs in on Susie Wiles cancer diagnosis
items.standfirst: President Trump announced Monday that White House chief of staff Susie Wiles was diagnosed with early-stage breast cancer. Andy Card, who was White House chief of staff for former President George W. Bush, joins CBS News with more.
items.content: President Trump announced Monday that White House chief of staff Susie Wiles was diagnosed with early-stage breast cancer. Andy Card, who was White House chief of staff for former President George W. Bush, joins CBS News with more.

clusters.title: Cubans abroad will be able to invest, own businesses on island
clusters.neutral_headline: Cuba Allows Nationals Abroad To Invest

items.title: Cubans abroad will be able to invest, own businesses on island
items.standfirst: Cubans living abroad will now be allowed to invest and own businesses on the island, a senior government official said Monday, unveiling a key reform as the economy struggles under intense US pressure.
items.content: Cubans living abroad will now be allowed to invest and own businesses on the island, a senior government official said Monday, unveiling a key reform as the economy struggles under intense US pressure.

items.title: Cuba will allow nationals abroad to invest in and own businesses on the island, economic czar says
items.standfirst: Cuban nationals living abroad in places such as Miami will be allowed to invest in the private sector and own businesses in their homeland, the country's economic czar has told NBC News in an exclusive interview.
items.content: Cuban nationals living abroad in places such as Miami will be allowed to invest in the private sector and own businesses in their homeland, the country's economic czar has told NBC News in an exclusive interview.

ai_summary: Cubans living abroad can invest on the island
Can own businesses in their homeland
Economic reform unveiled by government
Allows investment in the private sector
Nationals abroad can participate in economy

items.title: A Trump official quits over the Iran war, as Israel says it killed 2 Iranian commanders
items.standfirst: Israel says it killed Ali Larijani and Gholamreza Soleimani. Iran has yet to confirm but it would be the highest-profile killings since the targeting of Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
items.content: Israel says it killed Ali Larijani and Gholamreza Soleimani. Iran has yet to confirm but it would be the highest-profile killings since the targeting of Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)

ai_summary: Soleimani was a senior Iranian official
Killed in the US Israeli war on Iran
Israel says it killed two Iranian commanders
Iran has not confirmed the killings
Soleimani was a commander of Basij forces

clusters.title: Britain hit by ‘unprecedented’ meningitis outbreak, two students dead
clusters.neutral_headline: Britain Hit By Unprecedented Meningitis Outbreak

items.title: Britain hit by ‘unprecedented’ meningitis outbreak, two students dead
items.standfirst: Britain has been hit by an “unprecedented” meningitis outbreak, the country’s health minister warned Tuesday after two students died from catching a rare bacterial strain of the disease. So far, 15 cases have been reported. The outbreak has been linked to a Canterbury nightclub.
items.content: Britain has been hit by an “unprecedented” meningitis outbreak, the country’s health minister warned Tuesday after two students died from catching a rare bacterial strain of the disease. So far, 15 cases have been reported. The outbreak has been linked to a Canterbury nightclub.
